highlights.text: chronic inflammatory interstitial pneumonitis presents for follow-up.
most likely to be observed in the biopsy specimen compared to a healthy individual?
Chronic inflammation can lead to fibrosis (scarring) and changes in the lung architecture.
n chronic inflammatory diseases, there may be thinning of the alveolar walls due to atrophy or destruction of Type I pneumocytes.
n chronic inflammation, these cells may proliferate and become hypertrophic, and their number may increase to compensate for damage to Type I cells.
. In chronic interstitial pneumonitis, an increase in fibroblasts and collagen deposition leading to fibrosis is common.
